<type>file</type>
<content>1: # Model: OpenCode Fork
2: 
3: **Domain:** Dylan's OpenCode fork at `~/Documents/personal/opencode`
4: **Created:** 2026-02-13
5: **Source:** Investigation `2026-02-13-inv-build-model-opencode-fork.md` + fork's own `2026-02-11-inv-opencode-fork-resource-audit-investigate.md`
6: 
7: ---
8: 
9: ## Summary
10: 
11: Dylan owns a fork of [sst/opencode](https://github.com/sst/opencode) at `~/Documents/personal/opencode`. The fork is 13 custom commits ahead of upstream (linear forward — all upstream changes included). Custom changes focus on **memory management** (LRU/TTL instance eviction preventing 8.4GB unbounded growth), **SSE cleanup** (idempotent teardown preventing leaked connections), **OAuth stealth mode** (Claude Max access), and **ORCH_WORKER header forwarding** (worker session detection). The fork is a TypeScript monorepo (Bun runtime, Hono HTTP framework) with sessions stored as JSON files on disk — no database. Session status (idle/busy/retry) is tracked in-memory only, lost on server restart. A `GET /session/status` endpoint already exists for querying session state.
12: 
13: ---
14: 
15: ## Architecture
16: 
17: ### Tech Stack
18: - **Language:** TypeScript (ES modules, strict Zod validation)
19: - **Runtime:** Bun 1.3.5+
20: - **HTTP Framework:** Hono 4.10.7 with OpenAPI descriptions
21: - **AI SDK:** Vercel AI SDK 5.0 with providers for Anthropic, Google, OpenAI, Bedrock, etc.
22: - **Frontend:** Solid.js 1.9.10 + TailwindCSS 4.1 + Kobalte UI
23: - **Build:** Turbo monorepo, Vite for frontend
24: - **IDs:** ULID-based descending identifiers (newer sessions = lower IDs)
25: 
26: ### Monorepo Structure
27: ```
28: packages/
29: ├── opencode/     # Core server + CLI + TUI (the main package)
30: ├── sdk/          # JavaScript SDK (client library for API)
31: ├── app/          # Web UI (Solid.js SPA)
32: ├── web/          # Marketing/docs site
33: ├── desktop/      # Desktop app wrapper
34: ├── plugin/       # Plugin system
35: ├── ui/           # Shared UI components
36: ├── util/         # Shared utilities
37: └── ...           # enterprise, containers, slack, etc.
38: ```
39: 
40: ### Session Storage
41: Sessions are JSON files on disk at `~/.local/share/opencode/storage/`:
42: ```
43: session/{projectID}/{sessionID}.json    # Session metadata
44: message/{sessionID}/{messageID}.json    # Messages per session
45: part/{messageID}/{partID}.json          # Message parts (text, tool calls, etc.)
46: session_diff/{sessionID}.json           # File diffs (migrated out of session object)
47: ```
48: 
49: - **No database** — pure filesystem with file locking (read/write locks via `Lock`)
50: - **Project scoping** — sessions grouped by `projectID` (git root commit hash)
51: - **Migration system** — numbered migrations for storage format changes
52: - **Linear scan for list** — no indexing, scans all session files
53: 
54: ### Instance vs Session (Critical Distinction)
55: - **Instance** = execution context for a project directory (spawns LSP servers, MCP clients, file watchers)
56: - **Session** = conversation record (messages, parts, metadata) — persists as JSON files
57: - Instance has TTL/eviction; Session does NOT — sessions persist forever until explicitly deleted
58: - Multiple sessions can share one Instance (same project directory)
59: 
60: ---
61: 
62: ## Fork Divergence
63: 
64: ### Remote Setup
65: ```
66: fork     → git@github.com:dylan-conlin/opencode.git  (Dylan's GitHub fork)
67: origin   → https://github.com/sst/opencode            (upstream, stale Jan 27)
68: upstream → https://github.com/sst/opencode.git         (upstream, fetched Feb 5)
69: ```
70: 
71: ### Custom Commits (upstream/dev..HEAD, 13 commits)
72: 
73: | Date | Commit | Category | Description |
74: |------|--------|----------|-------------|
75: | Feb 11 | c08ed9c | investigation | Complete OpenCode fork resource audit |
76: | Feb 11 | fac8fc8 | investigation | Initial checkpoint for fork resource audit |
77: | Feb 9 | 5032a89 | fix(config) | Skip .test.ts/.spec.ts from plugin directories |
78: | Feb 9 | 26d2312 | fix(server) | Catch unhandled AbortError from prompt_async cancellation |
79: | Feb 7 | e9e4834 | fix | **Instance eviction (LRU/TTL) + SSE cleanup** — prevents 8.4GB growth |
80: | Feb 7 | c9c2796 | fix(server) | Bound instance cache with LRU eviction, fix SSE teardown on dispose |
81: | Feb 7 | 85b0bc8 | feat(server) | Log periodic memory usage for leak diagnosis |
82: | Feb 7 | 0d841ae | fix(tui) | Clean up error rendering for DOMException and NotFoundError |
83: | Jan 29 | 87e9212 | fix(sdk) | Make SSE reconnection fix permanent in build process |
84: | Jan 29 | 2a2c3bf | investigation | Verify OAuth priority fix was lost from dev branch |
85: | Jan 28 | 2e851f3 | fix | Forward ORCH_WORKER env var as x-opencode-env-ORCH_WORKER header |
86: | Jan 28 | f321338 | test | Tests for session.metadata.role from ORCH_WORKER header |
87: | Jan 28 | 2137108 | feat(stealth) | **Full pi-ai stealth mode** parity for Claude Max OAuth |
88: 
89: ### Sync Strategy
90: - Last upstream fetch: Feb 5, 2026
91: - Sync method: `git reset --moving to upstream/dev` + cherry-pick custom commits
92: - upstream/dev IS ancestor of local dev — pure linear forward advancement
93: - **510 commits ahead of origin/dev** (stale origin, not meaningfully different from upstream)
94: 
95: ### Critical Custom Changes
96: 
97: **1. Instance LRU/TTL Eviction (instance.ts)**
98: Upstream: 95-line file, bare `Map<string, Promise<Context>>()` — zero eviction, unbounded growth.
99: Fork: 350-line file with `MAX_INSTANCES=20`, `IDLE_TTL_MS=30min`, LRU eviction, TTL eviction, active-instance protection, eviction counters. Prevents the 8.4GB catastrophe that was killing macOS.
100: 
101: **2. SSE Cleanup (server.ts)**
102: Added idempotent SSE teardown with `cleaned` flag and dual cleanup paths (client abort + server dispose). Prevents leaked SSE connections from accumulating memory.
103: 
104: **3. OAuth Stealth Mode**
105: Implements pi-ai stealth mode parity for Claude Max OAuth. Enables Opus access via Max subscription.
106: 
107: **4. ORCH_WORKER Header (sdk/client.ts)**
108: Forwards `ORCH_WORKER` env var as `x-opencode-env-ORCH_WORKER` HTTP header. Enables server to detect worker sessions. **Note:** This change is in `packages/sdk/js/src/v2/client.ts` and the commit message references `session.metadata.role` being set on the server side (session.ts:207-211), but the corresponding server-side code was not found in the current codebase — may have been lost during an upstream rebase.
109: 
110: ---
111: 
112: ## Session Management
113: 
114: ### Session Schema (Zod-validated)
115: ```typescript
116: Session.Info = {
117:   id: string                    // Descending ULID (newer = lower)
118:   slug: string                  // URL-safe slug
119:   projectID: string             // Git root commit hash
120:   directory: string             // Working directory path
121:   parentID?: string             // For forked sessions
122:   title: string                 // User-visible title
123:   version: string               // OpenCode version
124:   time: {
125:     created: number             // Timestamp (ms)
126:     updated: number             // Timestamp (ms)
127:     compacting?: number         // If compaction in progress
128:     archived?: number           // Archive timestamp (manual flag)
129:   }
130:   summary?: { additions, deletions, files }
131:   share?: { url: string }       // Shareable link
132:   permission?: PermissionRuleset
133:   revert?: { messageID, partID?, snapshot?, diff? }
134: }
135: ```
136: 
137: **Key observations:**
138: - **No `metadata` bag** — schema is fixed fields only
139: - **No TTL/expiry field** — sessions persist forever
140: - **No state field** — idle/busy tracked separately in memory
141: - **`time.archived`** is a manual flag, not auto-expiry
142: 
143: ### Session Status (In-Memory Only)
144: ```typescript
145: SessionStatus.Info =
146:   | { type: "idle" }
147:   | { type: "retry", attempt: number, message: string, next: number }
148:   | { type: "busy" }
149: ```
150: - Stored in `Record<string, Info>` keyed by sessionID
151: - Created via `Instance.state()` — tied to Instance lifecycle, **lost on server restart**
152: - Idle sessions are deleted from the map (absence = idle)
153: - Published via `session.status` Bus event → SSE stream
154: 
155: ### Session Lifecycle
156: 1. **Create** → JSON file written to `session/{projectID}/{sessionID}.json`
157: 2. **Prompt** → Status set to "busy", messages/parts appended incrementally
158: 3. **Complete** → Status set to "idle" (deleted from status map), Instance stays cached
159: 4. **Archive** → Manual `time.archived` flag via PATCH, hides from active list
160: 5. **Delete** → Recursive deletion of session + all messages + all parts + unshare
161: 
162: ---
163: 
164: ## API Surface (Session-Relevant Endpoints)
165: 
166: ### REST API (Hono, OpenAPI-documented)
167: 
168: | Method | Endpoint | OperationId | Notes |
169: |--------|----------|-------------|-------|
170: | GET | `/session` | session.list | Filters: directory, roots, start, search, limit |
171: | GET | `/session/status` | session.status | **Returns all session statuses (idle/busy/retry)** |
172: | GET | `/session/:id` | session.get | Single session metadata |
173: | POST | `/session` | session.create | Input: parentID?, title?, permission? |
174: | PATCH | `/session/:id` | session.update | **Only title and time.archived** |
175: | DELETE | `/session/:id` | session.delete | Recursive delete (session + messages + parts) |
176: | POST | `/session/:id/message` | session.prompt | Streaming response |
177: | POST | `/session/:id/prompt_async` | session.prompt_async | Fire-and-forget (204) |
178: | POST | `/session/:id/abort` | session.abort | Cancel active prompt |
179: | GET | `/session/:id/message` | session.messages | All messages with parts |
180: | GET | `/event` | global.event | **SSE stream (all bus events)** |
181: | GET | `/health` | global.health | Server health + version |
182: | POST | `/dispose` | global.dispose | Dispose all instances |
183: 
184: ### SSE Events (via GET /event)
185: ```
186: session.created, session.updated, session.deleted
187: session.status (idle/busy/retry changes)
188: session.diff, session.error
189: message.updated, message.removed
190: part.updated, part.removed
191: server.heartbeat (every 30s)
192: server.instance.disposed
193: ```
194: 
195: ---
196: 
197: ## Feasibility: Three Proposed Features
198: 
199: ### Feature 1: Session TTL with Configurable Expiry
200:

<content>1: # Model: Orchestration Cost Economics
2: 
3: **Domain:** Agent Orchestration / Model Selection / Cost Management
4: **Last Updated:** 2026-01-28
5: **Synthesized From:** 15 investigations, 25+ kb quick entries, and decisions spanning Nov 2025 - Jan 2026
6: 
7: ---
8: 
9: ## Summary (30 seconds)
10: 
11: Agent orchestration cost is driven by three factors: **model pricing** (10-100x variance), **access restrictions** (fingerprinting, OAuth blocking), and **visibility** (lack of tracking caused $402 surprise spend). The Jan 2026 cost crisis revealed that headless spawning without cost visibility leads to runaway spend. DeepSeek V3 at $0.25/$0.38/MTok is now a **viable primary option** after testing confirmed stable function calling (contradicting earlier "unstable" documentation).
12: 
13: ---
14: 
15: ## The Cost Problem Timeline
16: 
17: | Date | Event | Impact |
18: |------|-------|--------|
19: | Dec 2025 | Gemini Flash (free) as default | $0/mo, hit 2,000 req/min TPM limit |
20: | Jan 9, 2026 | Switch to Sonnet API (pay-per-token) | Unknown cost, no tracking |
21: | Jan 9, 2026 | Anthropic blocks OAuth for third-party tools | Can't use Max subscription via OpenCode |
22: | Jan 12, 2026 | Investigation identifies cost tracking need | Never implemented |
23: | Jan 13, 2026 | Cancel second Max subscription | $2,400/year savings |
24: | Jan 18, 2026 | Discover $402 spent in ~2 weeks | $70-80/day burn rate |
25: | Jan 18, 2026 | Switch back to Max subscription default | $200/mo flat |
26: | Jan 19, 2026 | Test confirms DeepSeek V3 function calling works | New viable option |
27: 
28: ---
29: 
30: ## Model Pricing Comparison
31: 
32: | Model | Input $/MTok | Output $/MTok | Access Method | Notes |
33: |-------|--------------|---------------|---------------|-------|
34: | **DeepSeek V3** | $0.25 | $0.38 | API | **10-65x cheaper, function calling works** |
35: | DeepSeek R1 | $0.45 | $2.15 | API | Reasoning model, function calling experimental |
36: | Gemini Flash | ~$0.10-0.30 | Variable | API/Free tier | 2,000 req/min limit blocks tool-heavy agents |
37: | Claude Haiku | $1.00 | $5.00 | API | Fast, lightweight |
38: | Claude Sonnet | $3.00 | $15.00 | API | Doubles at >200K context |
39: | Claude Opus | $5.00 | $25.00 | API or Max+CLI | Highest quality |
40: | **Claude Max** | $200/mo flat | - | Claude CLI only | Unlimited Sonnet + Opus |
41: 
42: ### Cost Equivalence Points
43: 
44: At **$200/mo Max subscription breakeven:**
45: - DeepSeek V3: ~317M input tokens OR ~526M output tokens
46: - Sonnet: ~67M input tokens OR ~13M output tokens
47: - Opus: ~40M input tokens OR ~8M output tokens
48: 
49: **Key insight:** At Jan 18 burn rate ($70-80/day with Sonnet), Max subscription is 10x cheaper. But DeepSeek V3 could be even cheaper if function calling holds up.
50: 
51: ---
52: 
53: ## Internal Credit System (Reverse Engineered Jan 2026)
54: 
55: **Source:** she-llac.com/claude-limits - obtained via reverse engineering SSE responses and Stern-Brocot tree algorithm on float precision artifacts. Data may become stale if Anthropic patches the leak.
56: 
57: ### Credit Formula
58: 
59: Anthropic uses an internal credit system to meter subscription usage:
60: 
61: ```
62: credits_used = ceil(input_tokens × input_rate + output_tokens × output_rate)
63: ```
64: 
65: **Per-model credit rates:**
66: 
67: | Model | Input Rate | Output Rate |
68: |-------|------------|-------------|
69: | Haiku | 2/15 (0.133) | 10/15 (0.667) |
70: | Sonnet | 6/15 (0.4) | 30/15 (2.0) |
71: | Opus | 10/15 (0.667) | 50/15 (3.333) |
72: 
73: ### Actual Limits vs Marketing Claims
74: 
75: | Plan | Marketing | 5-Hour Session | Weekly Limit | Actual Ratio |
76: |------|-----------|----------------|--------------|--------------|
77: | Pro ($20) | 1× | 550,000 | 5,000,000 | baseline |
78: | Max 5× ($100) | 5× | 3,300,000 | 41,666,700 | **6× session, 8.33× weekly** |
79: | Max 20× ($200) | 20× | 11,000,000 | 83,333,300 | **20× session, 16.67× weekly** |
80: 
81: **Key insight:** Max 5× actually **overdelivers** (6× session, 8.33× weekly) while Max 20× **underdelivers** on weekly (16.67× instead of 20×). The 5× plan may offer better value per dollar.
82: 
83: ### Cache Pricing: Subscription Advantage
84: 
85: | Operation | API Cost | Subscription Cost |
86: |-----------|----------|-------------------|
87: | Cache read | 10% of input rate | **FREE** |
88: | Cache write (5-min) | 1.25× input rate | Regular input price |
89: | Cache write (1-hour) | 2× input rate | Regular input price |
90: 
91: **This is massive for agentic work:** Our tool-heavy orchestration (dozens of tool calls per agent turn) generates heavy cache hits. Free cache reads mean warm-cache scenarios deliver up to **36× value over API**.
92: 
93: ### Value Multipliers (API Equivalent)
94: 
95: | Plan | Monthly Cost | API Equivalent Value | Multiplier |
96: |------|-------------|---------------------|------------|
97: | Pro | $20 | $163 | 8.1× |
98: | Max 5× | $100 | $1,354 | 13.5× |
99: | Max 20× | $200 | $2,708 | 13.5× |
100: 
101: **Validation:** Our Jan 18 discovery showed $70-80/day API burn rate (~$2,100-2,400/mo). At 13.5× value multiplier, our $200/mo Max subscription delivers ~$2,708 equivalent API value - right at our burn rate. External validation of cost decision.
102: 
103: ---
104: 
105: ## Access Restrictions
106: 
107: ### Anthropic Fingerprinting (Jan 2026)
108: 
109: **What's blocked:**
110: - Third-party tools (OpenCode, Cursor) using Max subscription OAuth
111: - Opus 4.5 via any non-Claude-Code path
112: 
113: **How it works:**
114: - TLS fingerprinting (JA3)
115: - HTTP/2 frame characteristics
116: - Tool name patterns (lowercase vs PascalCase+_tool)
117: - OAuth scope requirements
118: 
119: **Evidence:** All community workarounds failed within 6 hours. Anthropic iterates faster than bypasses can stabilize.
120: 
121: **Source:** `.kb/investigations/archived/2026-01-08-inv-opus-auth-gate-fingerprinting.md`, `.kb/investigations/archived/2026-01-09-inv-anthropic-oauth-community-workarounds.md`
122: 
123: #### Failed Bypass Timeline (Jan 8-9, 2026)
124: 
125: | Time | Attempt | Result |
126: |------|---------|--------|
127: | Jan 8 PM | Opus 4.5 fingerprint spoofing | Failed - "authorized for use with Claude Code only" |
128: | Jan 8 PM | Direct header injection | Failed - sophisticated detection, broke other models |
129: | Jan 9 AM | opencode-anthropic-auth@0.0.7 plugin | Worked briefly, then blocked |
130: | Jan 9 PM | Community coordination attempts | All failed within hours |
131: 
132: **Conclusion:** Cat-and-mouse is not a viable strategy. Anthropic's detection is actively maintained.
133: 
134: ### Rate Limits vs Usage Quota (Critical Distinction)
135: 
136: **⚠️ There are TWO distinct limit types, and they behave differently:**
137: 
138: | Limit Type | Scope | Docker Bypass? | Resets |
139: |------------|-------|----------------|--------|
140: | **Request-rate limits** | Device (Statsig fingerprint) | ✅ YES | Immediately with new fingerprint |
141: | **Weekly usage quota** | Account | ❌ NO | Weekly (Sunday) |
142: 
143: **Request-rate limits:** Per-device throttling based on Statsig fingerprint. Fresh Docker container = fresh fingerprint = no throttling.
144: 
145: **Weekly usage quota:** Account-level weekly allocation (e.g., "97% used"). Tied to account, not device. Docker escape hatch does NOT bypass this.
146: 
147: **Test evidence (Jan 20-21):**
148: - Wiped `~/.claude-docker/`, logged in as gmail account
149: - Usage charged to gmail (2%→3%) while sendcutsend stayed at 94-95%
150: - Copying Statsig fingerprint did NOT bypass 97% quota
151: 
152: **When to use Docker escape hatch:**
153: - ✅ Request-rate limited (getting throttled, need to spawn immediately)
154: - ❌ Weekly quota exhausted (must wait for reset or switch accounts)
155: 
156: **Source:** `kb-e3e0a8`, `kb-c3dbe7`, `.kb/investigations/2026-01-21-inv-synthesize-anthropic-blocking-kb-quick.md`
157: 
158: ### Cross-Account Rate Limit Bug
159: 
160: **Problem:** Device fingerprinting causes one account's rate limit to affect other accounts on same device.
161: 
162: **Status:** Known bug since March 2025 (GitHub #630), unfixed.
163: 
164: **Workaround:** Docker container (confirmed working for rate limits, not for quota).
165: 
166: **Source:** `~/.kb/investigations/2025-11-30-claude-code-cross-account-rate-limit-bug.md`
167: 
168: ---
169: 
170: ## DeepSeek V3: Viable Primary Option
171: 
172: ### Previous Understanding (Jan 18)
173: 
174: > "The current version of the deepseek-chat model's Function Calling capability is unstable, which may result in looped calls or empty responses." - DeepSeek API docs
175: 
176: **Recommendation at the time:** Do not use for agentic work.
177: 
178: ### Current Understanding (Jan 19)
179: 
180: **Test performed:** Spawned DeepSeek V3 agent via OpenCode API with investigation skill.
181: 
182: **Tool calls executed successfully:**
183: - Read (3 files)
184: - Grep (17 matches found)
185: - Bash (ran go test)
186: - Write (created investigation file)
187: - kb quick constrain (externalized knowledge)
188: 
189: **Result:** 3 minutes, 62K tokens, successful completion with SYNTHESIS.md.
190: 
191: **Conclusion:** DeepSeek V3 function calling is stable enough for standard orchestration tasks. The "unstable" warning may be outdated or apply only to edge cases.
192: 
193: ### Revised Recommendation
194: 
195: | Workload | Recommended Model | Cost |
196: |----------|-------------------|------|
197: | Standard investigation/feature work | DeepSeek V3 | $0.25/$0.38/MTok |
198: | Complex reasoning, architecture | Claude Opus (via Max) | $200/mo flat |
199: | Tool-heavy bursts (>2000 req/min) | Sonnet API or Max | Variable |
200: | Cost-insensitive, highest quality | Claude Opus | $5/$25/MTok |

(File has more lines. Use 'offset' parameter to read beyond line 200)
</content>

state.output:
<path>/Users/dylanconlin/Documents/personal/orch-go/.kb/models/opencode-fork.md</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>200: 
201: **Current state:** Sessions persist forever. Instance has TTL (30min); sessions do not. Archive is manual.
202: 
203: **What needs to change:**
204: 1. Add `ttl?: number` field to `Session.Info.time` schema (Zod)
205: 2. Add periodic cleanup job (new — nothing like this exists today)
206: 3. Cleanup job scans sessions, deletes those where `Date.now() - time.updated > ttl`
207: 4. Make TTL configurable — either per-session (set at creation) or global via env var
208: 5. Update `POST /session` create input to accept TTL
209: 6. SSE event on auto-deletion so orch-go can react
210: 
211: **Effort:** MODERATE (2-4 hours)
212: - Storage layer is simple (JSON files), no schema migration needed
213: - Periodic job is new pattern (no scheduler for sessions exists today)
214: - Instance eviction pattern in `instance.ts` is a good template
215: - Needs careful handling: don't delete sessions with active prompts
216: 
217: **Risk:** LOW
218: - Schema addition is backward-compatible (optional field)
219: - Cleanup job is independent, doesn't interfere with existing code paths
220: 
221: ### Feature 2: Session Metadata API
222: 
223: **Current state:** `Session.Info` has fixed Zod schema. No extensible metadata bag. PATCH only supports `title` and `time.archived`. The ORCH_WORKER header forwarding was an attempt at metadata but the server-side code appears lost.
224: 
225: **What needs to change:**
226: 1. Add `metadata?: Record<string, string>` field to `Session.Info` schema
227: 2. Accept `metadata` in `POST /session` (create) and `PATCH /session/:id` (update)
228: 3. Update `Session.createNext()` to include metadata in initial write
229: 4. Update `Session.update()` PATCH handler to merge metadata
230: 5. Metadata persists to disk (just another field in the JSON file)
231: 
232: **Effort:** LOW (1-2 hours)
233: - Adding an optional field to a Zod schema is trivial
234: - Storage is JSON — no migration needed, old sessions just lack the field
235: - PATCH endpoint already has the pattern for partial updates
236: - SDK client needs update to pass metadata in requests
237: 
238: **Risk:** VERY LOW
239: - Backward-compatible — old sessions without metadata still valid
240: - No performance impact (tiny additional JSON field)
241: 
242: **orch-go usage:** Store `beads_id`, `workspace_path`, `tier`, `spawn_mode` with session at creation. Query via `GET /session/:id` — eliminates workspace `.session_id` cross-reference files entirely.
243: 
244: ### Feature 3: Session State HTTP Endpoint
245: 
246: **Current state:** `GET /session/status` ALREADY EXISTS. Returns `Record<string, SessionStatus>` where status is `idle | busy | retry`. This is exactly what orch-go needs.
247: 
248: **What needs to change:**
249: 1. **Nothing on the OpenCode side** — endpoint already works
250: 2. On orch-go side: use `GET /session/status` instead of SSE-only polling
251: 3. Optionally: add per-session `GET /session/:id/status` endpoint for targeted queries
252: 
253: **Effort:** NEAR-ZERO for OpenCode (endpoint exists), LOW for orch-go integration
254: 
255: **Risk:** VERY LOW
256: - Read-only endpoint, no side effects
257: - Already has OpenAPI documentation
258: 
259: **Caveat:** Status is in-memory only — lost on server restart. All sessions appear "idle" after restart. This is acceptable for orch-go since it can treat "no status info" as "check if session exists" fallback.
260: 
261: ---
262: 
263: ## Constraints and Gotchas
264: 
265: ### Sync with Upstream
266: - Fork must be periodically rebased onto upstream/dev
267: - Custom changes can be lost during rebase (ORCH_WORKER server-side code was lost)
268: - Strategy: keep custom commits minimal and well-isolated
269: - **13 custom commits is manageable** — any more and rebasing becomes painful
270: 
271: ### Instance vs Session Confusion
272: - Instance eviction (TTL/LRU) does NOT delete sessions
273: - Session deletion does NOT dispose instances
274: - These are independent lifecycles with different storage (memory vs disk)
275: - orch-go currently conflates "session exists" with "agent is running" — need to check status, not just session existence
276: 
277: ### Status is Ephemeral
278: - `SessionStatus` lives in-memory only via `Instance.state()`
279: - Server restart = all status lost = all sessions appear idle
280: - No persistence of busy/idle transitions
281: - SSE `session.status` events are the only way to track transitions in real-time
282: 
283: ### Storage is Linear Scan
284: - `Session.list()` iterates all session JSON files (no index)
285: - `Session.children()` iterates all sessions to find matching `parentID`
286: - Works fine for <1000 sessions but could become slow with very high session counts
287: - No query optimization possible without adding an index layer
288: 
289: ### PATCH Endpoint is Limited
290: - Only accepts `title` and `time.archived` today
291: - Adding metadata support requires updating the Zod validator in `session.ts:264-274`
292: - Must update both the route validator AND the update function body
293: 
294: ---
295: 
296: ## What This Enables (for orch-go lifecycle)
297: 
298: | orch-go Problem | OpenCode Feature | Lines Eliminated |
299: |-----------------|-----------------|------------------|
300: | Ghost/phantom/orphan cleanup | Session TTL auto-expiry | ~1,200 (cleanup logic) |
301: | Workspace .session_id cross-reference | Session metadata API | ~800 (file-based cross-ref) |
302: | SSE-only status polling | GET /session/status (exists!) | ~1,400 (SSE status parsing) |
303: | **Total** | | **~3,400 lines** |
304: 
305: ### Implementation Priority
306: 1. **Session metadata API** — lowest effort, highest immediate value (eliminates cross-ref files)
307: 2. **GET /session/status integration** — zero OpenCode work, orch-go client change only
308: 3. **Session TTL** — most complex but largest cleanup elimination
309: 
310: ---
311: 
312: ## What This Model Enables / Constrains
313: 
314: **Enables:**
315: - Planning concrete implementation of the three features in the fork
316: - Understanding what orch-go changes depend on what OpenCode changes
317: - Estimating effort accurately (hours, not days)
318: 
319: **Constrains:**
320: - Must keep custom commits minimal (rebase cost)
321: - Must not break upstream compatibility (fork may re-sync)
322: - Session metadata schema must be generic (not orch-go-specific field names in OpenCode)
323: - TTL cleanup must respect active prompts (don't delete mid-conversation)
324: 

(End of file - total 324 lines)
</content>

204: ---
205: 
206: ## Cost Visibility Gap
207: 
208: ### The Problem
209: 
210: Switched from free Gemini to paid Sonnet on Jan 9 with **no cost tracking**:
211: - Dashboard shows Max subscription usage (OAuth)
212: - Dashboard does NOT show API token usage
213: - No per-spawn cost visibility
214: - No budget alerts
215: 
216: **Result:** $402 spent in ~2 weeks without awareness, ramping to $70-80/day.
217: 
218: ### The Solution (Not Yet Implemented)
219: 
220: **Hybrid approach recommended:**
221: 1. **Local token counting** - Calculate from OpenCode session metadata, per-spawn granularity
222: 2. **Anthropic Usage API** - `/v1/billing/cost` for ground truth (returns "Not Found" currently)
223: 3. **Budget alerts** - Warn at 80%, critical at 95%, auto-switch at 100%
224: 
225: **Pricing for local calculation (Sonnet 4.5):**
226: - Input: $3.00/MTok
227: - Output: $15.00/MTok
228: - Cache write: $3.75/MTok
229: - Cache read: $0.30/MTok
230: 
231: **Source:** `.kb/investigations/archived/2026-01-12-inv-sonnet-cost-tracking-requirements.md`, `.kb/investigations/2026-01-18-inv-add-api-cost-tracking-widget.md`
232: 
233: ---
234: 
235: ## Spawn Path Economics
236: 
237: ### Current Architecture (Dual Spawn)
238: 
239: | Path | Backend | Models | Cost | Use When |
240: |------|---------|--------|------|----------|
241: | **Primary** | OpenCode API | Sonnet, DeepSeek, Gemini | Pay-per-token | Normal work |
242: | **Escape Hatch** | Claude CLI | Opus, Sonnet (Max) | $200/mo flat | Infrastructure, high-quality |
243: 
244: ### Economic Decision Tree
245: 
246: ```
247: Is this infrastructure work (orch, opencode, spawn system)?
248:   → YES: Use escape hatch (Claude CLI + Max) - independence matters
249:   → NO: Continue...
250: 
251: Is cost the primary constraint?
252:   → YES: Use DeepSeek V3 ($0.25/$0.38/MTok)
253:   → NO: Continue...
254: 
255: Is highest quality needed?
256:   → YES: Use Opus via Max subscription
257:   → NO: Use Sonnet or DeepSeek V3
258: ```
259: 
260: **Source:** `.kb/models/model-access-spawn-paths.md`, `.kb/decisions/2026-01-18-max-subscription-primary-spawn-path.md`
261: 
262: ---
263: 
264: ## Alternatives Evaluated
265: 
266: ### OpenAI ChatGPT Pro ($200/mo)
267: 
268: - Codex CLI available (terminal agent like Claude Code)
269: - OAuth has bugs, less mature than Claude Code
270: - No API access included (same as Anthropic Max)
271: - Viable backup if Anthropic restricts further
272: 
273: **Source:** `.kb/investigations/archived/2026-01-18-inv-research-compare-openai-chatgpt-pro-anthropic-max.md`
274: 
275: ### OpenCode Zen (Cooperative Buying Pool)
276: 
277: - Claims breakeven pricing via volume pooling
278: - Sustainability uncertain (no financial transparency)
279: - Not recommended until funding model proven
280: 
281: ### OpenCode Black ($200/mo)
282: 
283: - Emergency response to Jan 9 Anthropic block
284: - Routes through "enterprise gateway" to bypass restrictions
285: - Temporary cat-and-mouse, not sustainable
286: - Treat as industry drama, not strategic option
287: 
288: **Source:** `.kb/investigations/archived/2026-01-13-research-opencode-zen-black-architecture-economics.md`
289: 
290: ---
291: 
292: ## Strategic Questions Answered
293: 
294: ### Q: Is Sonnet API cheaper than Max subscription?
295: 
296: **A:** At normal usage, no. Jan 18 showed $70-80/day burn rate with Sonnet API → $2,100-2,400/mo projected. Max at $200/mo is 10x cheaper.
297: 
298: ### Q: Should we use DeepSeek for orchestration?
299: 
300: **A:** Yes, now that function calling is confirmed working. DeepSeek V3 at $0.25/$0.38/MTok is viable for most orchestration work. Reserve Opus for complex reasoning.
301: 
302: ### Q: When to use escape hatch (Max subscription)?
303: 
304: **A:** Infrastructure work (fixing orch/opencode itself), complex architecture decisions, or when API is rate-limited.
305: 
306: ### Q: Is second Max subscription needed?
307: 
308: **A:** No. Cancelled Jan 13. Opus gate forced lighter consumption patterns. Can re-subscribe in 5 minutes if needed.
309: 
310: ---
311: 
312: ## Constraints
313: 
314: ### C1: Anthropic Protects Claude Code Revenue
315: 
316: Fingerprinting blocks third-party tools from Max subscription. This is intentional product strategy, not a bug. Expect continued enforcement.
317: 
318: ### C2: Cost Visibility Required Before API Usage
319: 
320: The $402 surprise proves: never use pay-per-token without cost tracking. Implement tracking before returning to Sonnet API as default.
321: 
322: ### C3: Model Quality vs Task Complexity
323: 
324: **Benchmark caveat:** SWE-bench scores (R1: 49.2% vs Opus: 80.9%) measure complex multi-file coding, not general orchestration. Most orchestration tasks (investigation, search, file reading) don't require SWE-bench-level capability.
325: 
326: **Practical guidance:**
327: - Standard investigation/search → DeepSeek V3 sufficient (tested Jan 19)
328: - Complex multi-file refactoring → Prefer Claude
329: - Architecture decisions → Prefer Opus (reasoning quality matters)
330: 
331: ### C4: Gemini Flash TPM Limits Block Tool-Heavy Work
332: 
333: 2,000 req/min limit hit by single investigation agent (35+ tool calls/sec). Not viable for primary orchestration.
334: 
335: ---
